signals.title: Claude Managed Agents webhooks , multiagent orchestration , and self-hosted sandboxes are now available on Claude Platfo
signals.summary: Claude Managed Agents webhooks , multiagent orchestration , and self-hosted sandboxes are now available on Claude Platform on AWS.
signals.rawContentSnippet: Claude Managed Agents webhooks , multiagent orchestration , and self-hosted sandboxes are now available on Claude Platform on AWS . See IAM actions for Claude Platform on AWS for the new IAM actions and the AnthropicSelfHostedEnvironmentAccess managed policy.

signals.title: We've launched Claude Opus 4.8 ( claude-opus-4-8 ), our most capable generally available model.
signals.summary: 8 ( claude-opus-4-8 ), our most capable generally available model.
signals.rawContentSnippet: We've launched Claude Opus 4.8 ( claude-opus-4-8 ), our most capable generally available model. Claude Opus 4.8 supports a 1M token context window by default, 128k max output tokens, and the same set of tools and platform features as Claude Opus 4.7. See What's new in Claude Opus 4.8 for capability improvements, new features, and migration guidance.

signals.title: We've launched mid-conversation system messages .
signals.summary: We've launched mid-conversation system messages.
signals.rawContentSnippet: We've launched mid-conversation system messages . On Claude Opus 4.8 , you can send role: "system" messages at non-first positions in the messages array, preserving prompt cache hits when instructions change during a long-running session. No beta header is required.

signals.title: The Messages API now returns refusal categories in stop_details when Claude Opus 4.8 declines a request, so your applica
signals.summary: The Messages API now returns refusal categories in stop_details when Claude Opus 4.
signals.rawContentSnippet: The Messages API now returns refusal categories in stop_details when Claude Opus 4.8 declines a request, so your application can route different classes of refusal to the right next step. No beta header is required. See Refusal categories .

signals.title: On Claude Opus 4.8 , the effort parameter defaults to high across all surfaces, including Claude Code and the Messages A
signals.summary: 8 , the effort parameter defaults to high across all surfaces, including Claude Code and the Messages API.
signals.rawContentSnippet: On Claude Opus 4.8 , the effort parameter defaults to high across all surfaces, including Claude Code and the Messages API.

signals.title: Claude Opus 4.8 uses adaptive thinking to trigger reasoning only when a turn needs it, reducing wasted thinking tokens c
signals.summary: 8 uses adaptive thinking to trigger reasoning only when a turn needs it, reducing wasted thinking tokens compared to Claude Opus 4.
signals.rawContentSnippet: Claude Opus 4.8 uses adaptive thinking to trigger reasoning only when a turn needs it, reducing wasted thinking tokens compared to Claude Opus 4.7 at the same effort level.

signals.title: The advisor tool now supports Claude Opus 4.8 .
signals.summary: The advisor tool now supports Claude Opus 4.
signals.rawContentSnippet: The advisor tool now supports Claude Opus 4.8 .

signals.title: Fast mode for Claude Opus 4.8 is available as a research preview on the Claude API only.
signals.summary: 8 is available as a research preview on the Claude API only.
signals.rawContentSnippet: Fast mode for Claude Opus 4.8 is available as a research preview on the Claude API only.

signals.title: In Claude Code, Workflows are available as a research preview, letting you define and run multi-step agentic plans.
signals.summary: In Claude Code, Workflows are available as a research preview, letting you define and run multi-step agentic plans.
signals.rawContentSnippet: In Claude Code, Workflows are available as a research preview, letting you define and run multi-step agentic plans. See the Claude Code documentation .

signals.title: We've deprecated fast mode for Claude Opus 4.6, with removal approximately 30 days after launch.
signals.summary: We've deprecated fast mode for Claude Opus 4.
signals.rawContentSnippet: We've deprecated fast mode for Claude Opus 4.6, with removal approximately 30 days after launch. Migrate to fast mode for Claude Opus 4.8 or Claude Opus 4.7. Read more in Fast mode .

signals.title: Batch Skill Import
signals.summary: Batch Skill Import : Preview GitHub repo contents before installing.
signals.rawContentSnippet: Batch Skill Import : Preview GitHub repo contents before installing. Select and install multiple skills from the same repo in one go.

signals.title: Multi-Step Questionnaire
signals.summary: Multi-Step Questionnaire : Agent can present structured multi-step questions with single/multi-select options, free-text input, and images.
signals.rawContentSnippet: Multi-Step Questionnaire : Agent can present structured multi-step questions with single/multi-select options, free-text input, and images.

signals.title: Content Filter Graceful Fallback
signals.summary: Content Filter Graceful Fallback : When the model can’t complete a response, the original message returns to the input box with a friendly notice instead of an error.
signals.rawContentSnippet: Content Filter Graceful Fallback : When the model can’t complete a response, the original message returns to the input box with a friendly notice instead of an error. Rephrase and resend directly.

signals.title: Avatar Click-to-Upload
signals.summary: Avatar Click-to-Upload : Click the Agent avatar on the home page to upload a custom image.
signals.rawContentSnippet: Avatar Click-to-Upload : Click the Agent avatar on the home page to upload a custom image.

signals.title: Unified folder permissions
signals.summary: Unified folder permissions: one authorization covers read, write, edit, and search — no repeated prompts for the same folder.
signals.rawContentSnippet: Unified folder permissions: one authorization covers read, write, edit, and search — no repeated prompts for the same folder.

signals.title: Fixed Agent tool calls passing inaccurate parameters in some scenarios
signals.summary: Fixed Agent tool calls passing inaccurate parameters in some scenarios.
signals.rawContentSnippet: Fixed Agent tool calls passing inaccurate parameters in some scenarios.

signals.title: MCP tunnels is now available as a Research Preview, so you can connect to MCP servers in your private network.
signals.summary: MCP tunnels is now available as a Research Preview, so you can connect to MCP servers in your private network.
signals.rawContentSnippet: MCP tunnels is now available as a Research Preview, so you can connect to MCP servers in your private network.

signals.title: DeepSeek-V4 ​
signals.summary: The DeepSeek API now supports V4-Pro and V4-Flash, available via both the OpenAI ChatCompletions interface and the Anthropic interface.
signals.rawContentSnippet: The DeepSeek API now supports V4-Pro and V4-Flash, available via both the OpenAI ChatCompletions interface and the Anthropic interface. To access the new models, the base_url remains unchanged, and the model parameter should be set to deepseek-v4-pro or deepseek-v4-flash . The two legacy API model names, deepseek-chat and deepseek-reasoner , will be discontinued in three months (2026-07-24). During the current period, these two model names point to the non-thinking mode and thinking mode of deepseek-v4-flash , respectively. For more details, please refer to this documentation .

signals.title: Reasoning effort now defaults to medium
signals.summary: Reasoning effort now defaults to medium.
signals.rawContentSnippet: Reasoning effort now defaults to medium .

signals.title: When image_detail is unset or set to auto , the model now uses original behavior
signals.summary: When image_detail is unset or set to auto , the model now uses original behavior.
signals.rawContentSnippet: When image_detail is unset or set to auto , the model now uses original behavior .

signals.summary: Tool search in the Responses API, which lets models defer large tool surfaces until runtime to reduce token usage, preserve cache performance, and improve latency.
signals.rawContentSnippet: Tool search in the Responses API, which lets models defer large tool surfaces until runtime to reduce token usage, preserve cache performance, and improve latency.

signals.rawContentSnippet: Built-in Computer use support in GPT-5.4 through the Responses API computer tool for screenshot-based UI interaction.

signals.rawContentSnippet: A 1M token context window and native Compaction support for longer-running agent workflows.

signals.title: DeepSeek-V3.1-Terminus ​
signals.summary: Both deepseek-chat and deepseek-reasoner have been upgraded to DeepSeek-V3.
signals.rawContentSnippet: Both deepseek-chat and deepseek-reasoner have been upgraded to DeepSeek-V3.1-Terminus. deepseek-chat corresponds to DeepSeek-V3.1-Terminus's non-thinking mode , while deepseek-reasoner corresponds to its thinking mode . This update maintains the model's original capabilities while addressing issues reported by users, including: Language consistency: Reduced occurrences of Chinese-English mixing and occasional abnormal characters;

signals.title: DeepSeek-V3.1 ​
signals.summary: Both deepseek-chat and deepseek-reasoner have been upgraded to DeepSeek-V3.
signals.rawContentSnippet: Both deepseek-chat and deepseek-reasoner have been upgraded to DeepSeek-V3.1. deepseek-chat corresponds to DeepSeek-V3.1's non-thinking mode , while deepseek-reasoner corresponds to its thinking mode . Key updates in DeepSeek-V3.1: Hybrid reasoning architecture : A single model supports both thinking mode and non-thinking mode Improved reasoning efficiency : Compared to DeepSeek-R1-0528, DeepSeek-V3.1-Think provides answers in significantly less time

signals.title: deepseek-reasoner ​
signals.summary: deepseek-reasoner Model Upgraded to DeepSeek-R1-0528: Enhanced Reasoning Capabilities Significant benchmark improvements (Pass@1) AIME 2025: 70.
signals.rawContentSnippet: deepseek-reasoner Model Upgraded to DeepSeek-R1-0528: Enhanced Reasoning Capabilities Significant benchmark improvements (Pass@1) AIME 2025: 70.0 → 87.5 (+17.5) GPQA: 71.5 → 81.0 (+9.5)

signals.title: deepseek-chat ​
signals.summary: deepseek-chat Model Upgraded to DeepSeek-V3-0324: Enhanced Reasoning Capabilities Significant improvements in benchmark performance: MMLU-Pro: 75.
signals.rawContentSnippet: deepseek-chat Model Upgraded to DeepSeek-V3-0324: Enhanced Reasoning Capabilities Significant improvements in benchmark performance: MMLU-Pro: 75.9 → 81.2 (+5.3)

signals.title: deepseek-coder & deepseek-chat Upgraded to DeepSeek V2.5 Model ​
signals.summary: The DeepSeek V2 Chat and DeepSeek Coder V2 models have been merged and upgraded into the new model, DeepSeek V2.
signals.rawContentSnippet: The DeepSeek V2 Chat and DeepSeek Coder V2 models have been merged and upgraded into the new model, DeepSeek V2.5. For backward compatibility, API users can access the new model through either deepseek-coder or deepseek-chat . The new model significantly surpasses the previous versions in both general capabilities and code abilities.

signals.title: API Launches Context Caching on Disk Technology ​
signals.summary: The DeepSeek API has innovatively adopted hard disk caching, reducing prices by another order of magnitude.
signals.rawContentSnippet: The DeepSeek API has innovatively adopted hard disk caching, reducing prices by another order of magnitude. For more details on the update, please refer to the documentation Context Caching is Available 2024/08/02 .
